content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves gently to the left ahead with the dashed center line visible, and the car appears well-positioned on the track following the curve.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 62, target = (62 + 5) % 121 = 67
- Target WP67: (3.1679, 4.3986)
- Car: x=3.9104, y=3.7361, heading=133.72°
- dy = 4.3986 - 3.7361 = 0.6625
- dx = 3.1679 - 3.9104 = -0.7425
- bearing = atan2(0.6625, -0.7425) = 180° - atan2(0.6625, 0.7425) = 180° - 41.7° = 138.3°
- delta = 138.3° - 133.72° = +4.58° → nearly straight, tiny left

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Track curves gently left ahead, car is slightly right of center at heading 133.7°, delta to WP67 is +4.58° (just under 5°), so steering 0° to maintain speed and avoid oscillation.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Track curves left (southwest) from WP55-70, heading ~130-140°",
    "Positive steering = left turn, negative = right turn",
    "Delta under 5° means steer 0 to avoid oscillation",
    "Delta 5-15° use steering ±5°; 15-30° use ±10 to ±15°",
    "After WP67-70, track curves more sharply left heading south — prepare larger left steer",
    "Speed 1.5 is safe on moderate curves; reduce to 1.0 or 0.75 for sharp turns",
    "Car has been consistently making small corrections on southwest section",
    "Heading wraps near ±180° when traveling west — normalize delta carefully",
    "Car is slightly right of center (~0.09m) but all wheels on track, no correction needed",
    "WP67 is at (3.168, 4.399), bearing ~138.3° from current position"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [4.132, 4.070, 3.980, 3.910],
    "y": [3.516, 3.581, 3.670, 3.736],
    "yaw": [130.61, 131.33, 132.66, 133.72]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": "Target WP67 at (3.168, 4.399): dy=0.663, dx=-0.743, bearing=138.3°, delta=+4.58° → nearly straight, steer 0°",
  "speed": 1.5,
  "steering_angle": 0.0
}
```
